text.content: 1.0  Transition Into Exit Check  [s1_0_transition_into_exit_check]

rich_text.plain_text: 1.0  Transition Into Exit Check  [s1_0_transition_into_exit_check]

text.content: "You learned to choose scales. You checked if the biggest number fits. You checked the last digits. Let's see what you know."

rich_text.plain_text: "You learned to choose scales. You checked if the biggest number fits. You checked the last digits. Let's see what you know."

text.content: 1.1  Scale Too Small Identify  [s1_1_scale_too_small_identify]

rich_text.plain_text: 1.1  Scale Too Small Identify  [s1_1_scale_too_small_identify]

text.content: Vertical data table appears: Toys Collected. Amy: 45, Ben: 70, Cora: 15, Dan: 30.

rich_text.plain_text: Vertical data table appears: Toys Collected. Amy: 45, Ben: 70, Cora: 15, Dan: 30.

text.content: Four scale options displayed below the data table. Scale of 1, 2, and 5 marked with ❌ and 'doesn't fit'. Scale of 10 marked with ✓.

rich_text.plain_text: Four scale options displayed below the data table. Scale of 1, 2, and 5 marked with ❌ and 'doesn't fit'. Scale of 10 marked with ✓.

text.content: "Four friends collected toys for a donation drive. Ben collected the most with 70 toys. Which scale works best for this data?"

rich_text.plain_text: "Four friends collected toys for a donation drive. Ben collected the most with 70 toys. Which scale works best for this data?"

text.content:
tool: multiple_choice  options: ["Scale of 1", "Scale of 2", "Scale of 5", "Scale of 10"]
"Which scale works best for this data?"

rich_text.plain_text:
tool: multiple_choice  options: ["Scale of 1", "Scale of 2", "Scale of 5", "Scale of 10"]
"Which scale works best for this data?"

text.content: ✅ Student selected Scale of 10, correct

rich_text.plain_text: ✅ Student selected Scale of 10, correct

text.content: "That's right. Scale of 10. For data up to 70, scale of 2 would need too many lines and scale of 5 would need fewer, but still many lines. Scale of 10 only needs 8, much easier to read."

rich_text.plain_text: "That's right. Scale of 10. For data up to 70, scale of 2 would need too many lines and scale of 5 would need fewer, but still many lines. Scale of 10 only needs 8, much easier to read."

text.content: ❌ Student selected Scale of 1

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student selected Scale of 1

text.content: Scale of 1 option pulses briefly to acknowledge selection.

rich_text.plain_text: Scale of 1 option pulses briefly to acknowledge selection.

text.content: "Scale of 1 would take 70 tick marks to reach Ben's data. That's way too crowded. Look at the biggest number and think about how many lines you'd need."

rich_text.plain_text: "Scale of 1 would take 70 tick marks to reach Ben's data. That's way too crowded. Look at the biggest number and think about how many lines you'd need."

text.content: ❌ Student selected Scale of 2

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student selected Scale of 2

text.content: Scale of 2 option pulses briefly to acknowledge selection.

rich_text.plain_text: Scale of 2 option pulses briefly to acknowledge selection.

text.content: "Scale of 2 would work if the numbers were small, but to reach 70, you'd need 35 tick marks. That's a lot. Try a bigger scale."

rich_text.plain_text: "Scale of 2 would work if the numbers were small, but to reach 70, you'd need 35 tick marks. That's a lot. Try a bigger scale."

text.content: ❌ Student selected Scale of 5

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student selected Scale of 5

text.content: Scale of 5 option pulses briefly to acknowledge selection.

rich_text.plain_text: Scale of 5 option pulses briefly to acknowledge selection.

text.content: "Scale of 5 could fit the data, but to reach 70, you'd need 14 tick marks. There's a bigger scale that makes the graph even cleaner."

rich_text.plain_text: "Scale of 5 could fit the data, but to reach 70, you'd need 14 tick marks. There's a bigger scale that makes the graph even cleaner."

text.content: ❌ Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

text.content: Visual comparison shows all four scales side by side. Scale of 1 shows 70 marks crowded together. Scale of 2 shows 35 marks. Scale of 5 shows 14 marks. Scale of 10 shows 8 marks, cleanest and easiest to read.

rich_text.plain_text: Visual comparison shows all four scales side by side. Scale of 1 shows 70 marks crowded together. Scale of 2 shows 35 marks. Scale of 5 shows 14 marks. Scale of 10 shows 8 marks, cleanest and easiest to read.

text.content: "Let me show you why Scale of 10 works best here. The biggest number is 70. Scale of 1 would need 70 tick marks. That's way too many. Scale of 2 needs 35. Still crowded. Scale of 5 needs 14. Better, but we can do even cleaner. Scale of 10 only needs 8 tick marks to reach 70. That's the clearest graph. When data goes up to 70 and all the numbers are multiples of 5, Scale of 10 is your best choice."

rich_text.plain_text: "Let me show you why Scale of 10 works best here. The biggest number is 70. Scale of 1 would need 70 tick marks. That's way too many. Scale of 2 needs 35. Still crowded. Scale of 5 needs 14. Better, but we can do even cleaner. Scale of 10 only needs 8 tick marks to reach 70. That's the clearest graph. When data goes up to 70 and all the numbers are multiples of 5, Scale of 10 is your best choice."

text.content: 1.2  Non Multiples Need Scale 2  [s1_2_non_multiples_need_scale_2]

rich_text.plain_text: 1.2  Non Multiples Need Scale 2  [s1_2_non_multiples_need_scale_2]

text.content: Horizontal data table appears. Minutes Reading data. Gus: 23, Hal: 31, Eli: 9, Fay: 16.

rich_text.plain_text: Horizontal data table appears. Minutes Reading data. Gus: 23, Hal: 31, Eli: 9, Fay: 16.

text.content: "These students tracked how many minutes they read this week. Check the numbers: 23, 31, 9, 16. Which scale will show ALL these values exactly?"

rich_text.plain_text: "These students tracked how many minutes they read this week. Check the numbers: 23, 31, 9, 16. Which scale will show ALL these values exactly?"

text.content:
tool: multiple_choice  options: ["Scale of 2", "Scale of 5", "Scale of 10"]
"Which scale will show ALL these values exactly?"

rich_text.plain_text:
tool: multiple_choice  options: ["Scale of 2", "Scale of 5", "Scale of 10"]
"Which scale will show ALL these values exactly?"

text.content: ✅ Student selected Scale of 2, correct

rich_text.plain_text: ✅ Student selected Scale of 2, correct

text.content: "That's right. Scale of 2. The biggest value of 31 could work with Scale of 2 or Scale of 5. Then check those digits in the ones place: 3, 1, 9, 6. None are 0 or 5 so they aren't multiples of 5. That's your Scale of 2 signal to show the exact values on the graph."

rich_text.plain_text: "That's right. Scale of 2. The biggest value of 31 could work with Scale of 2 or Scale of 5. Then check those digits in the ones place: 3, 1, 9, 6. None are 0 or 5 so they aren't multiples of 5. That's your Scale of 2 signal to show the exact values on the graph."

text.content: ❌ Student selected Scale of 5

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student selected Scale of 5

text.content: All four values highlighted in data table.

rich_text.plain_text: All four values highlighted in data table.

text.content: "Scale of 5 works for multiples of 5. Check the ones place digits: 3, 1, 9, 6. Do any end in 0 or 5?"

rich_text.plain_text: "Scale of 5 works for multiples of 5. Check the ones place digits: 3, 1, 9, 6. Do any end in 0 or 5?"

text.content: ❌ Student selected Scale of 10

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student selected Scale of 10

text.content: 23 and 31 highlighted in data table.

rich_text.plain_text: 23 and 31 highlighted in data table.

text.content: "Scale of 10 only shows exact values for multiples of 10. Look at 23 and 31. Neither ends in 0, so where would they land on the graph?"

rich_text.plain_text: "Scale of 10 only shows exact values for multiples of 10. Look at 23 and 31. Neither ends in 0, so where would they land on the graph?"

text.content: ❌ Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

text.content: All four values highlighted in data table.

rich_text.plain_text: All four values highlighted in data table.

text.content: Ones place digits pulse: 3, 1, 9, 6.

rich_text.plain_text: Ones place digits pulse: 3, 1, 9, 6.

text.content: "Here's how to know. Look at the ones place: 3, 1, 9, 6. Not a single 0 or 5, so these numbers aren't multiples of 5. Scales of 5 and 10 won't show them exactly. But Scale of 2 can show any whole number exactly because half of 2 is 1. The answer is Scale of 2."

rich_text.plain_text: "Here's how to know. Look at the ones place: 3, 1, 9, 6. Not a single 0 or 5, so these numbers aren't multiples of 5. Scales of 5 and 10 won't show them exactly. But Scale of 2 can show any whole number exactly because half of 2 is 1. The answer is Scale of 2."

text.content: ✅ Student selected Scale of 10, correct

text.content: "Right. Scale of 10. For data up to 90, a scale of 10 gives you a clean graph with only 10 lines on the axis."

rich_text.plain_text: "Right. Scale of 10. For data up to 90, a scale of 10 gives you a clean graph with only 10 lines on the axis."

text.content: ❌ Student selected Scale of 5

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student selected Scale of 5

rich_text.plain_text: 90 (Cara) highlights in data table.

text.content: "Scale of 5 works, but look at 90. Count by 5s: you need 19 tick marks to reach it. Scale of 10 only needs 10. Which is easier to read?"

rich_text.plain_text: "Scale of 5 works, but look at 90. Count by 5s: you need 19 tick marks to reach it. Scale of 10 only needs 10. Which is easier to read?"

text.content: ❌ Student selected Scale of 2

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student selected Scale of 2

text.content: 90 (Cara) highlights in data table.

rich_text.plain_text: 90 (Cara) highlights in data table.

text.content: "The biggest number is 90. Scale of 2 means counting 0, 2, 4, 6, all the way to 90. That's 46 marks on the axis. Way too crowded. Try a bigger scale."

rich_text.plain_text: "The biggest number is 90. Scale of 2 means counting 0, 2, 4, 6, all the way to 90. That's 46 marks on the axis. Way too crowded. Try a bigger scale."

text.content: Vertical bar graph appears with Scale of 10. Steps Walked data displayed. Ana: 30, Ben: 70, Cara: 90, Dan: 50. Axis shows 0, 10, 20...100 with 11 tick marks.

rich_text.plain_text: Vertical bar graph appears with Scale of 10. Steps Walked data displayed. Ana: 30, Ben: 70, Cara: 90, Dan: 50. Axis shows 0, 10, 20...100 with 11 tick marks.

text.content: "Let me show you. The biggest number is 90. All values end in 0, so they're multiples of 10. With Scale of 10, every bar lands exactly on a line: 30 at 30, 50 at 50, 70 at 70, 90 at 90. The axis only needs 10 marks to reach 90. Clean and easy to read. For big numbers that are multiples of 10, Scale of 10 is the best choice."
